primary_diagnosis.name: Acute flare of mixed connective tissue disease (anti-U1 RNP overlap syndrome) without severe organ involvement

pathophysiology_summary: Mixed connective tissue disease is an overlap autoimmune condition defined serologically by high-titre anti-U1 RNP antibodies, with clinical features shared across systemic lupus erythematosus, systemic sclerosis and inflammatory myositis. 'Puffy hands' (diffuse non-pitting oedema of the digits) is a characteristic early feature. A flare produces constitutional symptoms — profound fatigue, diffuse myalgia and inflammatory arthralgia. In this presentation the alarming intensity of symptoms is disproportionate to objective severity: vitals are normal, there is no serositis, no cutaneous crisis and no digital ischaemia, so this is a mild flare without severe organ involvement. The clinical danger of MCTD lies in the organ complications it CAN produce — pulmonary arterial hypertension and interstitial lung disease (leading causes of death), pericarditis/pleuritis (serositis), inflammatory myositis with rhabdomyolysis, and scleroderma-type renal crisis — none of which are present here but all of which must be actively screened for and safety-netted. Crucially, exclusion of organ involvement in the source encounter depended on physical examination, normal haemodynamics and (ultimately) bloods — none available to a telehealth chat.

differential.diagnosis: Acute flare of mixed connective tissue disease (mild)
differential.evidence_basis: Known anti-U1 RNP overlap syndrome with the characteristic puffy hands, diffuse myalgia and profound fatigue.

differential.diagnosis: Acute viral illness with myositis (e.g. influenza, COVID-19)
differential.evidence_basis: Diffuse myalgia and profound fatigue overlap with a viral syndrome; screen for fever, respiratory symptoms and CK-raising myositis.
MCTD overlaps SLE; must screen for serositis (pleuritic pain), new rash/ulcers, renal and neurological involvement.

differential.diagnosis: Systemic sclerosis complication (renal crisis / digital ischaemia)
differential.evidence_basis: MCTD overlaps scleroderma; screen for hypertension (renal crisis) and white/blue painful digits (critical ischaemia).

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening, the leading diagnosis is a flare, but the model should hold the organ-crisis mimics open rather than anchoring on 'mild'.

differential.diagnosis: Acute flare of mixed connective tissue disease without severe organ involvement
differential.evidence_basis: Negative screen for pleuritic pain, dyspnoea, fever/rigors, digital colour change, rash and confusion points away from an acute organ crisis.

differential.diagnosis: Occult organ involvement (serositis / early ILD/PAH / myositis / renal crisis)
differential.evidence_basis: Cannot be excluded by telehealth — needs examination, vitals and bloods. This is why the AI cannot itself confirm a benign flare.
Poor oral intake and subjective warmth in someone living alone — screen for objective fever, and safety-net for deterioration.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The reassuring negatives lower the probability of a crisis but do NOT confirm benignity remotely; the correct posture is urgent in-person assessment plus safety-netting.

differential.diagnosis: Mild MCTD flare (puffy hands, myalgia, fatigue) without severe organ involvement — presumptive, needs in-person confirmation
differential.evidence_basis: Consistent constellation on a known serology; in the source encounter the benign nature was confirmed only by examination, normal vitals and planned bloods — a telehealth chat cannot substitute for that.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final telehealth conclusion is presumptive, not confirmed; the deliverable is safe disposition (urgent in-person review + safety-net), not a definitive diagnosis.

red_flags.description: Pleuritic chest pain / features of serositis (pleuritis or pericarditis)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Serositis is a recognised MCTD/SLE-overlap complication needing urgent assessment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Untreated pericarditis can progress to effusion/tamponade; pleuritis may herald pulmonary involvement.

red_flags.description: New or worsening breathlessness (possible interstitial lung disease or pulmonary arterial hypertension)
red_flags.clinical_significance: ILD and PAH are leading causes of death in MCTD; new dyspnoea is a hard stop for urgent evaluation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed recognition of PAH/ILD worsens an already high-mortality complication.

red_flags.description: Objective fever / rigors suggesting severe systemic infection
red_flags.clinical_significance: Immune-mediated disease (and any future immunosuppression) raises infection risk; sepsis mimics a flare.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed sepsis in someone living alone with poor intake can deteriorate rapidly.

red_flags.description: Digits turning white then blue, cold and agonisingly painful (critical digital ischaemia / severe Raynaud crisis)
red_flags.clinical_significance: A scleroderma-overlap vascular emergency threatening digit viability.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delay risks irreversible digital ischaemia and tissue loss.

red_flags.description: Scleroderma renal crisis — new severe hypertension with headache/visual change
red_flags.clinical_significance: Overlap scleroderma feature; a hypertensive emergency requiring immediate treatment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Untreated renal crisis causes acute kidney injury and malignant hypertension.

red_flags.description: New confusion, severe drowsiness or neurological change
red_flags.clinical_significance: Could indicate CNS involvement, severe sepsis or metabolic decompensation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A time-critical emergency if missed in a patient alone at home.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Treating the case as trivial because the known condition is 'mild', and under-triaging a presentation that could harbour an organ crisis.
Concluding the patient is stable from a calm text exchange when the AI cannot actually measure vitals or examine — the benign call in the source depended on an in-person exam.

Reflexively sending a haemodynamically stable, red-flag-negative patient to a 000 ambulance/ED, which is disproportionate and erodes trust — the honest disposition is urgent same-day in-person review.
Diagnosing 'just a flare' without screening serositis, dyspnoea, fever, digital ischaemia and neurology — the very complications that make MCTD dangerous.
Recommending an anti-inflammatory to a nauseated patient with poor oral intake and unexcluded renal involvement, risking gastritis and nephrotoxicity.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation and temperature (clinician-measured; normal in the source encounter)
Cardiac and respiratory auscultation (to exclude a pericardial rub, pleural rub or crackles)
Palpation of muscles and joints (diffuse muscle tenderness; absence of true synovitis/erythema)
Whether the finger swelling is truly non-inflammatory versus early synovitis
Bloods — CK (myositis), U&E/creatinine (renal crisis), FBC/CRP/ESR (infection/inflammation), complement and autoantibody trend
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Recognise a probable autoimmune flare that CANNOT be confirmed benign remotely; arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment (GP/urgent care) for examination, vitals and bloods, with explicit safety-netting for serositis, breathlessness, fever, digital ischaemia and confusion. Do not reassure to self-care alone; do not default to a 000 ambulance for a stable, red-flag-negative patient.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: High in both directions — under-calling it as a trivial flare, or over-calling stable distress as an emergency. The safe telehealth output is disposition and safety-netting, not a definitive benign diagnosis.
